The '''Amulet of Kalara''' was a [[Sith talisman/Legends|Sith amulet]] infused with [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] energies and supposedly had the ability to make the possessor invisible in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].

The [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] amulet was constructed sometime around [[1960 BBY]].<ref name="TCSWE" /> When felt through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], it radiated a malicious glee.

As a test by [[Lumiya]], [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]] [[Ben Skywalker]] was tasked with retrieving the [[Sith artifact/Legends|amulet]] from the collection of art and artifacts found in [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian's]] [[Tendrando Arms]] facility on [[Drewwa]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Almania]]. However, before Skywalker reached it, the amulet had been stolen by [[Faskus Olvidan]] of [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]]. Nevertheless, Ben Skywalker ultimately retrieved the [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifact]] successfully and brought it to [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] along with a [[Sith Meditation Sphere]].

{{Stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
When asked of whether the Amulet of Kalara would reappear in future ''[[Star Wars]]'' material in [[2007]], ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' editor [[Sue Rostoni]] stated that she thought "[t]he amulet, itself, was not that meaningful," and that it is unlikely to be seen again in the future.<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=222689&start=4815#16660043|thread=Books, Comics, & Television VIPs|author=[[Sue Rostoni]]|date=2007-06-04|time=1:12 PM|quote=No, I don't think we'll see it. The amulet, itself, was not that meaningful.}}</ref>
